devdoc.details: A Diamond facet for routing payments with fee distribution, security protections, and compliance rules.

SafeERC20FailedOperation(address).details: An operation with an ERC-20 token failed.

batchRoutePayments(address[],uint256[],a.details: All tokens must be supported or match primary token. Protected against DOS attacks.

params.amounts: Array of amounts corresponding to each token.
params.recipients: Array of recipients corresponding to each token.

params.recipient: Address of the recipient (kept for interface compatibility, not used in compliance check)

returns.isCompliant: Whether the sender satisfies claim topic requirements

returns.exists: Whether the claim topic is registered

deactivateFunctionCompliance(bytes32).details: Can be called by owner or trusted issuers

params.functionId: The identifier of the routing function

emergencyTokenRecovery(address,uint256,a.details: Should only be used if tokens get stuck due to failed transfers.

params.recipient: Address to send recovered tokens to.
params.token: Address of the token to recover.

returns.claimTopics: Array of all registered claim topics

params.functionId: The identifier of the routing function

returns.isActive: Whether the compliance requirement is active

returns.maxSize: The maximum number of transactions allowed in a single batch.

returns.threshold: The minimum fee threshold in basis points.

returns.functionIds: Array of all routing function identifiers

params.token: Address of the token to check.

returns.isSupported: True if token is supported (either as primary or has specific config).

reactivateFunctionCompliance(bytes32).details: Can be called by owner or trusted issuers

params.functionId: The identifier of the routing function

routePaymentWithFees(address,uint256,add.details: Uses the primary token configuration (backwards compatible).

params.recipient: Final recipient of the adjusted amount.
params.token: Address of ERC20 token being transferred.

routePaymentWithFeesAnyToken(address,uin.details: Uses token-specific configuration if available, falls back to primary token.

params.recipient: Final recipient of the adjusted amount.
params.token: Address of ERC20 token being transferred.

routePaymentWithFeesTokenOnly(address,ui.details: Automatically detects if token is supported, no fallback to primary token.

params.recipient: Final recipient of the adjusted amount.
params.token: Address of ERC20 token being transferred.

setFunctionClaimRequirements(bytes32,uin.details: Can be called by owner or trusted issuers. Uses centralized claim topics.

params.description: Human-readable description of the compliance requirement
params.functionId: The identifier of the routing function
params.requiredClaimTopics: Array of claim topics required for this function

devdoc.title: TransferFacet

MAX_BATCH_SIZE().notice: Maximum number of transactions allowed in batch operations to prevent DOS attacks

MIN_FEE_THRESHOLD().notice: Minimum fee threshold to prevent dust accumulation (0.0001% in basis points)

batchRoutePayments(address[],uint256[],a.notice: Batch route payments for multiple tokens.

checkCompliance(bytes32,address,address).notice: Checks if claim topic requirements are satisfied for a given sender

claimTopicExists(uint256).notice: Checks if a claim topic exists in the centralized registry

deactivateFunctionCompliance(bytes32).notice: Deactivates claim topic requirements for a specific routing function

emergencyTokenRecovery(address,uint256,a.notice: Emergency function to recover stuck tokens (only owner).

getAvailableClaimTopics().notice: Gets all available claim topics from the centralized registry

getFunctionClaimRequirements(bytes32).notice: Gets claim topic requirements for a specific routing function

getMaxBatchSize().notice: Gets the maximum batch size allowed for batch operations.

getMinFeeThreshold().notice: Gets the minimum fee threshold used to prevent dust.

getRoutingFunctionIds().notice: Gets all available routing function identifiers

getSupportedTokensForRouting().notice: Gets all tokens supported for routing.

isTokenSupportedForRouting(address).notice: Checks if a token is supported for routing.

reactivateFunctionCompliance(bytes32).notice: Reactivates claim topic requirements for a specific routing function

routePaymentWithFees(address,uint256,add.notice: Routes ERC20 payment from sender to recipient, applying fee distribution.

routePaymentWithFeesAnyToken(address,uin.notice: Routes any supported ERC20 token payment with fee distribution.

routePaymentWithFeesTokenOnly(address,ui.notice: Routes payment for any token that has fee configuration.

setFunctionClaimRequirements(bytes32,uin.notice: Sets claim topic requirements for a specific routing function
